optimize_0plus_b_sound.solution:
Fixpoint optimize_0plus_b (b : bexp) : bexp :=
  match b with
  | <{ true }> => <{ true }>
  | <{ false }> => <{ false }>
  | <{ a1 = a2 }> => <{ (optimize_0plus a1) = (optimize_0plus a2) }>
  | <{ a1 <= a2 }> => <{ (optimize_0plus a1) <= (optimize_0plus a2) }>
  | <{ ~ b1 }> => <{ ~ (optimize_0plus_b b1) }>
  | <{ b1 && b2 }> => <{ (optimize_0plus_b b1) && (optimize_0plus_b b2) }>
  end.

Theorem optimize_0plus_b_sound : forall b,
  beval (optimize_0plus_b b) = beval b.
Proof.
  induction b; simpl;
  try (rewrite optimize_0plus_sound; rewrite optimize_0plus_sound);
  try (rewrite IHb);
  try (rewrite IHb1; rewrite IHb2);
  reflexivity.
Qed.
optimize_0plus_b_sound.explanation: Extend 0+optimization to boolean expressions by recursively optimizing arithmetic subexpressions.

beval_rules.solution:
(* Evaluation rules for beval:
   BTrue:  beval <{ true }> = true
   BFalse: beval <{ false }> = false
   BEq:    beval <{ a1 = a2 }> = (aeval a1 =? aeval a2)
   BLe:    beval <{ a1 <= a2 }> = (aeval a1 <=? aeval a2)
   BNot:   beval <{ ~ b }> = negb (beval b)
   BAnd:   beval <{ b1 && b2 }> = (beval b1 && beval b2)
*)
beval_rules.explanation: The evaluation rules for boolean expressions follow directly from the Fixpoint definition of beval.

bevalR.solution:
Inductive bevalR : bexp -> bool -> Prop :=
  | E_BTrue : bevalR <{ true }> true
  | E_BFalse : bevalR <{ false }> false
  | E_BEq : forall a1 a2 n1 n2,
      aevalR a1 n1 -> aevalR a2 n2 ->
      bevalR <{ a1 = a2 }> (n1 =? n2)
  | E_BLe : forall a1 a2 n1 n2,
      aevalR a1 n1 -> aevalR a2 n2 ->
      bevalR <{ a1 <= a2 }> (n1 <=? n2)
  | E_BNot : forall b bv,
      bevalR b bv ->
      bevalR <{ ~ b }> (negb bv)
  | E_BAnd : forall b1 b2 bv1 bv2,
      bevalR b1 bv1 -> bevalR b2 bv2 ->
      bevalR <{ b1 && b2 }> (bv1 && bv2).

Lemma bevalR_iff_beval : forall b bv,
  bevalR b bv <-> beval b = bv.
Proof.
  split.
  - intros H. induction H; simpl;
    try (rewrite aevalR_iff_aeval in *; subst; reflexivity);
    try (subst; reflexivity).
  - generalize dependent bv. induction b; intros; simpl in *; subst;
    try constructor;
    try (apply aevalR_iff_aeval; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb1; reflexivity);
    try (apply IHb2; reflexivity).
Qed.
bevalR.explanation: Define relational evaluation for boolean expressions and prove equivalence with the functional version.

ceval_example2.solution:
Example ceval_example2:
  empty_st =[
    X := 0;
    Y := 1;
    Z := 2
  ]=> (Z !-> 2 ; Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
Proof.
  apply E_Seq with (X !-> 0).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 1 ; X !-> 0).
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
Qed.
ceval_example2.explanation: Demonstrate evaluation of a sequence of three assignments using the evaluation rules.

pup_to_n.solution:
Definition pup_to_n : com :=
  <{ Y := 0;
     while X <> 0 do
       Y := Y + X;
       X := X - 1
     end }>.

Theorem pup_to_2_ceval :
  (X !-> 2) =[
    pup_to_n
  ]=> (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
Proof.
  unfold pup_to_n.
  apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
  - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
    + reflexivity.
    + apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
      * apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_WhileTrue with (X !-> 0 ; Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
      * reflexivity.
      * apply E_Seq with (Y !-> 3 ; X !-> 1 ; Y !-> 2 ; Y !-> 0 ; X !-> 2).
        { apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. }
        { apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. }
      * apply E_WhileFalse. reflexivity.
Qed.
pup_to_n.explanation: Sum from X down to 0 into Y. Demonstrate evaluation starting with X=2.

XtimesYinZ_spec.solution:
(* Informal specification:
   Theorem XtimesYinZ_spec : forall st n m st',
     st X = n -> st Y = m ->
     st =[ XtimesYinZ ]=> st' ->
     st' Z = n * m.
*)
XtimesYinZ_spec.explanation: XtimesYinZ assigns Z := X * Y, so the final state has Z = n * m.

loop_never_stops.solution:
Theorem loop_never_stops : forall st st',
  ~(st =[ loop ]=> st').
Proof.
  intros st st' contra. unfold loop in contra.
  remember <{ while true do skip end }> as loopdef eqn:Heqloopdef.
  induction contra; try discriminate.
  - injection Heqloopdef as H1 H2. rewrite H1 in H. simpl in H. discriminate.
  - injection Heqloopdef as H1 H2. apply IHcontra2. rewrite H2. reflexivity.
Qed.
loop_never_stops.explanation: The infinite loop while true do skip end never terminates. Prove by induction on the evaluation derivation.

no_whiles_eqv.solution:
Inductive no_whilesR : com -> Prop :=
  | NW_Skip : no_whilesR <{ skip }>
  | NW_Asgn : forall x a, no_whilesR <{ x := a }>
  | NW_Seq : forall c1 c2, no_whilesR c1 -> no_whilesR c2 -> no_whilesR <{ c1 ; c2 }>
  | NW_If : forall b c1 c2, no_whilesR c1 -> no_whilesR c2 -> no_whilesR <{ if b then c1 else c2 end }>.

Theorem no_whiles_eqv:
  forall c, no_whiles c = true <-> no_whilesR c.
Proof.
  split.
  - induction c; intros H; simpl in *; try discriminate;
    try constructor;
    try (apply andb_true_iff in H; destruct H; auto).
  - intros H. induction H; simpl; try reflexivity;
    try (apply andb_true_iff; split; auto).
Qed.
no_whiles_eqv.explanation: Define a relational version of no_whiles and prove equivalence with the boolean version.

no_whiles_terminating.solution:
(* Informal proof:
   Theorem no_whiles_terminating : forall c st,
     no_whilesR c -> exists st', st =[ c ]=> st'.
   Proof by induction on c:
   - skip: trivial, st' = st
   - x := a: st' = (x !-> aeval st a ; st)
   - c1;c2: by IH, c1 terminates producing st1, then c2 terminates from st1
   - if b then c1 else c2: case split on beval st b, apply IH to appropriate branch
   - while: contradicts no_whilesR
*)
no_whiles_terminating.explanation: Programs without while loops always terminate. Prove by structural induction on the no_whilesR derivation.

stack_compiler.solution:
Fixpoint s_execute (st : state) (stack : list nat)
  (prog : list sinstr) : list nat :=
  match prog with
  | [] => stack
  | i :: prog' =>
    match i with
    | SPush n => s_execute st (n :: stack) prog'
    | SLoad x => s_execute st (st x :: stack) prog'
    | SPlus =>
      match stack with
      | n2 :: n1 :: stack' => s_execute st ((n1 + n2) :: stack') prog'
      | _ => s_execute st stack prog'
      end
    | SMinus =>
      match stack with
      | n2 :: n1 :: stack' => s_execute st ((n1 - n2) :: stack') prog'
      | _ => s_execute st stack prog'
      end
    | SMult =>
      match stack with
      | n2 :: n1 :: stack' => s_execute st ((n1 * n2) :: stack') prog'
      | _ => s_execute st stack prog'
      end
    end
  end.

Example s_execute1 :
  s_execute empty_st []
    [SPush 5; SPush 3; SPush 1; SMinus] = [2; 5].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Example s_execute2 :
  s_execute (X !-> 3) [3;4]
    [SPush 4; SLoad X; SMult; SPlus] = [15; 4].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.

Fixpoint s_compile (e : aexp) : list sinstr :=
  match e with
  | ANum n => [SPush n]
  | AId x => [SLoad x]
  | <{ a1 + a2 }> => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SPlus]
  | <{ a1 - a2 }> => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMinus]
  | <{ a1 * a2 }> => s_compile a1 ++ s_compile a2 ++ [SMult]
  end.

Example s_compile1 :
  s_compile <{ X - (2 * Y) }> =
  [SLoad X; SPush 2; SLoad Y; SMult; SMinus].
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
stack_compiler.explanation: Stack machine executor and compiler for arithmetic expressions.

execute_app.solution:
Theorem execute_app : forall st p1 p2 stack,
  s_execute st stack (p1 ++ p2) = s_execute st (s_execute st stack p1) p2.
Proof.
  intros st p1. induction p1 as [|i p1' IH]; intros; simpl.
  - reflexivity.
  - destruct i; try (apply IH);
    destruct stack as [|n1 [|n2 stack']]; try (apply IH).
Qed.
execute_app.explanation: Executing concatenated programs is equivalent to executing the first then the second.

stack_compiler_correct.solution:
Lemma s_compile_correct_aux : forall st e stack,
  s_execute st stack (s_compile e) = aeval st e :: stack.
Proof.
  intros st e. induction e; intros stack; simpl;
  try reflexivity;
  repeat rewrite execute_app;
  rewrite IHe1; rewrite IHe2; simpl; reflexivity.
Qed.

Theorem s_compile_correct : forall (st : state) (e : aexp),
  s_execute st [] (s_compile e) = [aeval st e].
Proof.
  intros. apply s_compile_correct_aux.
Qed.
stack_compiler_correct.explanation: The compiler is correct: executing compiled code produces the evaluation result. Proved via a generalized auxiliary lemma about arbitrary stacks.

short_circuit.solution:
(* Optional exercise - best effort:
   Add short-circuit evaluation for boolean && and ||.
   When the first operand of && is false, skip evaluating the second.
   When the first operand of || is true, skip evaluating the second.
   This requires modifying beval to use if-then-else instead of Coq's &&.
*)
short_circuit.explanation: Short-circuit evaluation optimization for boolean operators.

break_imp.solution:
Theorem break_ignore : forall c st st' s,
  st =[ c ]=> (st', s) ->
  st =[ c;; skip ]=> (st', s).
Proof.
  intros. destruct s.
  - apply E_SeqContinue with st'. assumption. apply E_Skip.
  - apply E_SeqBreak. assumption.
Qed.

Theorem while_continue : forall b c st st' s,
  st =[ while b do c end ]=> (st', s) ->
  s = SContinue.
Proof.
  intros. remember <{ while b do c end }> as loop.
  induction H; try discriminate; injection Heqloop; intros; subst; auto.
Qed.

Theorem while_stops_on_break : forall b c st st',
  beval st b = true ->
  st =[ c ]=> (st', SBreak) ->
  st =[ while b do c end ]=> (st', SContinue).
Proof.
  intros. apply E_WhileBreak; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_continue : forall c1 c2 st st' st'',
  st =[ c1 ]=> (st', SContinue) ->
  st' =[ c2 ]=> (st'', SContinue) ->
  st =[ c1;; c2 ]=> (st'', SContinue).
Proof.
  intros. apply E_SeqContinue with st'; assumption.
Qed.

Theorem seq_stops_on_break : forall c1 c2 st st',
  st =[ c1 ]=> (st', SBreak) ->
  st =[ c1;; c2 ]=> (st', SBreak).
Proof.
  intros. apply E_SeqBreak. assumption.
Qed.
break_imp.explanation: Properties of the break extension for Imp: while always returns SContinue, break stops execution, etc.

while_break_true.solution:
Theorem while_break_true : forall b c st st',
  st =[ CWhile b c ]=> st' / SContinue ->
  beval st' b = true ->
  exists st'', st'' =[ c ]=> st' / SBreak.
Proof.
  intros b c st st' H Hb.
  remember (CWhile b c) as loop.
  induction H; try discriminate.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. rewrite H in Hb. discriminate.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. exists st. assumption.
  - injection Heqloop as Hb' Hc'. subst. apply IHceval2; [reflexivity | assumption].
Qed.
while_break_true.explanation: Induction on the while evaluation. If guard is false, contradiction. If body breaks, that is our witness. If body continues, recurse on the remaining loop iterations.

ceval_deterministic.solution:
Theorem ceval_deterministic: forall (c:com) st st1 st2 s1 s2,
     st =[ c ]=> st1 / s1 ->
     st =[ c ]=> st2 / s2 ->
     st1 = st2 /\ s1 = s2.
Proof.
  intros c st st1 st2 s1 s2 E1 E2.
  generalize dependent s2. generalize dependent st2.
  induction E1; intros st2 s2 E2; inversion E2; subst;
    try (split; reflexivity);
    try (rewrite H in *; discriminate).
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H2. destruct H2. subst. split; reflexivity.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H1. destruct H1. subst.
    apply IHE1_2. assumption.
  - apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H6. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H6. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H6. assumption.
  - rewrite H in H4. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. subst. split; reflexivity.
  - apply IHE1 in H5. destruct H5. discriminate.
  - rewrite H in H2. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H3. destruct H3. discriminate.
  - apply IHE1_1 in H1. destruct H1. subst.
    apply IHE1_2. assumption.
Qed.
ceval_deterministic.explanation: Determinism for the break-extended language: induction on E1, inversion on E2, using IHs to equate intermediate states and signals.

add_for_loop.solution:
(* Design exercise: Add C-style for loops to Imp.

   Syntax:
     CFor (init : com) (guard : bexp) (update : com) (body : com)

   Semantics (as a derived form):
     for(init; guard; update) { body }
   is equivalent to:
     init; while guard do body; update end

   Add to com:
     | CFor (c_init : com) (b : bexp) (c_update : com) (c_body : com)

   Add evaluation rules:
     | E_For : forall st st' c_init b c_update c_body,
         st =[ c_init; while b do c_body; c_update end ]=> st' ->
         st =[ CFor c_init b c_update c_body ]=> st'
*)
add_for_loop.explanation: Design exercise: C-style for loops can be desugared to init; while guard do body; update end. Add CFor to com and E_For that delegates to the sequence+while translation.

optimize.solution:
(* Example: optimize away multiplication by 1 *)
Fixpoint optimize_mult1 (a : aexp) : aexp :=
  match a with
  | ANum n => ANum n
  | AId x => AId x
  | APlus a1 a2 => APlus (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  | AMinus a1 a2 => AMinus (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  | AMult (ANum 1) a2 => optimize_mult1 a2
  | AMult a1 (ANum 1) => optimize_mult1 a1
  | AMult a1 a2 => AMult (optimize_mult1 a1) (optimize_mult1 a2)
  end.

Theorem optimize_mult1_sound : forall a st,
  aeval st (optimize_mult1 a) = aeval st a.
Proof.
  induction a; simpl; try reflexivity; try (rewrite IHa1; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity).
  - (* AMult *) destruct a1; try (simpl; rewrite IHa2; destruct n; try reflexivity; simpl;
      destruct n; try (simpl; rewrite Nat.add_0_r; reflexivity); simpl; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity);
    try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; rewrite IHa2; reflexivity).
    destruct a2; try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; reflexivity);
    try (simpl; rewrite IHa1; reflexivity).
Qed.
optimize.explanation: Design exercise: one possible optimization eliminates multiplication by 1. The proof proceeds by structural induction on the arithmetic expression, with case analysis on AMult.
